narrative: OMX30 faces a technically muddled open with weak ADX (5.2) signaling low trend conviction despite a 6.7% bull-regime premium to 200-MA. Global cues are contradictory: Asian strength (Nikkei +138bps, Shanghai +166bps) battles US equity weakness (Dow -109bps, S&P -28bps), while copper surges +268bps and gold jumps +121bps—typical risk-off/commodity rotation. US 10Y yields climbed 88bps to 4.569%, which should help Nordic banks but pressures real estate and tech. Geopolitical noise (Iran strikes) and weak ADX bias suggest range-bound trading; RSI at 48 offers no mean-reversion edge. Confidence is deliberately suppressed to Low per weak ADX rule (reduce by 30%).
morningBrief: OMX30 opens Thursday in limbo: strong bull-regime technicals (+6.7% vs 200-MA) clash with anemic ADX (5.2), MACD below signal, and bearish DI tilt—classic low-conviction setup. Global macro split (Asia up, US down) and geopolitical tension (US-Iran cycle) keep overnight risk appetite muted. Copper's 2.7% rally favors Boliden and mining/industrials; gold +121bps is a bonus. Rising yields (+88bps) are a net positive for SEB, Handelsbanken, Swedbank (NIM expansion), but headwind for Fastighets Balder and real estate refinance costs. Watch 3123 (yesterday close); support at 3085 (lower Bollinger), resistance at 3180 (50-MA). Trade defensively; jobless claims at 14:30 CET could re-set risk tone. Avoid size into ADX this weak.
drivers: Weak ADX (5.2) reduces directional conviction across all signals
Mixed global macro: US yields +88bps, Nikkei +1.38%, but Dow -1.09% signals caution
Copper strength (+2.68%) supports industrials/mining, but oil weakness (-0.85%) offsets
Geopolitical overhang (US-Iran escalation) keeps risk appetite subdued despite gold rally

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y +88bps to 4.569% expands NIM outlook; SEK weakness vs USD (USD/SEK -0.27%) neutral to slight positive on lending rates.
Copper +2.68% and gold +1.21% provide direct revenue uplift; commodity rally typical of risk-off rotation.
Copper strength supports capital goods demand; USD/SEK -0.27% slightly negative for export competitiveness but contained.
Rising US yields (+88bps) increase refinance and WACC costs for leveraged property portfolios.
NASDAQ +0.20% overnight but S&P weakness tempers bullish momentum; yield rise pressures DCF multiples slightly.
EUR/SEK flat (-0.03%) eases sourcing cost pressure, but oil weakness (-0.85%) is minor positive for input costs; risk-off mood pressures discretionary.

watchList.reason: Copper +268bps and gold +121bps create strong tailwinds; commodity rally is principal support in weak macro.
US 10Y +88bps improves NIM; high correlation to rate moves makes it a lever on yield momentum.
Rising yields directly compress property valuations; refinance risk in Q3/Q4.
Copper rally supports demand, but geopolitical risk and weak ADX limit conviction. Hold range bias.
ADX 5.2 = weak trend; MACD below signal; RSI 48 neutral. Expect consolidation 3085–3180 until conviction returns.

keyLevels.bull_breakout: 3200 (above resistance signals sustained rally; requires ADX >18)
keyLevels.bear_breakdown: 3050 (below support signals mean-reversion risk, MACD cross looms)

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims (initial) at 14:30 CET today. Expected +240k; surprises could reignite yield volatility and repricing of risk sentiment. No major Swedish data calendar.
topRisk: Escalation in US-Iran cycle (second day of strikes) could spike VIX (currently 16.72, -106bps) and force broad risk-off rotation into defensives and commodities. Weak ADX means OMX30 has minimal anchoring; fast unwind of long carry trades possible. Monitor news flow closely pre-jobless claims.
technicalSummary: Bull regime: +6.7% vs 200-MA, +1.2% vs 50-MA, well-supported longer-term
Weak ADX (5.2) with bearish DI bias = no conviction, reduce confidence by 30% per rules
MACD below signal, histogram negative (-2.953) = early momentum fade
RSI 48.07 = dead neutral, no mean-reversion setup
Bollinger %B=0.47 = mid-range, width 5.31% = typical volatility, lower half bias suggests slight bearish lean
ROC-90: -1.3% neutral, 52W at 89% of range = upside is limited near-term
